title: lithocardium bonplandii
intro: Lithocardium bonplandii, also known as Digitalis purpurea, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the foxglove family (Scrophulariaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include the presence of cardiac glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin. These compounds have been found to have a positive effect on heart health, reducing symptoms of heart failure, atrial fibrillation, and other cardiovascular conditions. Lithocardium bonplandii is commonly prepared in herbal remedies such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including heart disease, and is often used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
